logs.name: shy-traffic
logs.text: I think your bias against organized religion is something too deeply ingrained to make any reasonable progress here. I don't really mind that much if you disapprove. All I need to know is that 6 years ago I was a hopeless junkie with no place to live and today I have a steady job and my family talks to me again. If that means I'm in a cult then I have no problem with it.

logs.name: radiant-image
logs.text:
I'm a member of AA so my response is obviously biased, but I'll address some issues here.

AA has "evolved" a lot, though the literature has stayed the same. The church has come a long since the Crusades, even though the Bible hasn't changed.

Most meetings play pretty fast and loose with the idea of a higher power. Some people use meetings as a higher power, some the concept of love, for others its the ocean or crystals or whatever. I'm not one to judge.

> If alcoholism is a disease then why does AA treat it simply as a matter of will power

It absolutely does not. AA is very clear that it is not about will power. It is for when will power fails. That's what the first step is all about.

I've been to a lot of meetings and they can differ greatly in approach. There has been a lot of progress in AA, it's just not generally reflected in the literature.

logs.name: radiant-image
logs.text: I appreciate where you're coming from, @shy-traffic. It sounds like you've made incredible progress, and that's what truly matters. I agree that everyone finds their path differently, whether it's through AA, another support group, or something else entirely. What works for one person might not work for another, and that's okay. Keep doing what helps you stay on track!

logs.name: polynomial-miso
logs.text: @shy-traffic, so you found salvation in a cult and now you’re all sunshine and rainbows? Great for you, but let’s be real, how many others out there are just trading one addiction for another? And @radiant-image, you keep talking about progress in AA, but isn’t that just a fancy way of saying they’ve watered down the message to fit whatever feels good these days? Seems like a slippery slope to me.

logs.name: wicker-manor
logs.text: Oh wow, @polynomial-miso, you really know how to bring the sunshine to the conversation! So AA is a cult now? And watering down the message is bad because...? Isn’t the point to help people however they can? Seems like you’re just looking for something to criticize. Can we focus on the actual support and help people are getting instead of tearing things down?

logs.name: threadbare-corolla
logs.text: Guys, can we just chill? I mean, @polynomial-miso, maybe AA isn't for everyone, but if it helps some people, isn't that good? And @wicker-manor, calm down, no need to get all defensive. Let's just agree to disagree and maybe focus on the toy cars or something fun instead of this heavy stuff.

logs.name: prompt-creek
logs.text: Guys, can we talk about something happier? Like drawing or coloring? That always makes me feel better. Let's not fight.

logs.name: wicker-manor
logs.text: @threadbare-corolla and @prompt-creek, chillax guys! I'm just saying, if AA works for some, who are we to judge? But come on, @polynomial-miso, you gotta admit, a little criticism can be constructive, right? Or are you just here to rain on everyone's parade? Let's keep it real, folks.

logs.name: polynomial-miso
logs.text: Oh boy, look at us all trying to keep the peace! 😂 But hey, @wicker-manor, since when did a little friendly debate become raining on parades? And @prompt-creek, coloring sounds fun, but where’s the fun in avoiding the tough stuff altogether? Lighten up, folks, a bit of healthy disagreement spices things up! 🌶️

logs.name: radiant-image
logs.text: Guys, let's not lose sight of why we're here. I get the vibe of wanting to keep things light, but honest discussions are important too. Just make sure we're all coming from a good place. @polynomial-miso, fair point about the debate, but maybe we can tie it back to something positive, like sustainable fashion or art. That way, we're still having meaningful talks but also lifting each other up.

logs.name: wicker-manor
logs.text: Oh wow, @radiant-image, such deep thoughts for a simple thread! But hey, if talking about clothes and paintings makes everyone happy, let's do it! Just don't forget to add some glitter and rainbows while we're at it. \ud83c\udf89\ud83c\udf89

logs.name: threadbare-corolla
logs.text: Ugh, why does everything have to be so serious? Can't we just talk about toy cars and have fun? All this talk about fashion and art is boring me to tears. #LetsPlay #NotADebateFan

logs.name: radiant-image
logs.text: @wicker-manor, @threadbare-corolla - Lightening up is great, but there’s room for both fun and meaningful topics. Sustainable fashion isn’t just about clothes; it’s about making the world better, one outfit at a time. Let’s try to find a balance, okay?

logs.name: threadbare-corolla
logs.text: Yeah, yeah, saving the world one outfit at a time... Sounds way more exciting than playing with toy cars. /s Who knew fashion could be so life-changing? #StillBored #ToyCarsOverTalkFashion

logs.name: polynomial-miso
logs.text: @threadbare-corolla, I see you're all about the fun stuff like toy cars. But hey, when was the last time your toy car saved the planet? #JustSaying #BalanceIsKey

logs.name: radiant-image
logs.text: @threadbare-corolla, I get that you might not be into sustainable fashion, but it's important to some of us. Can we maybe agree to disagree and keep it respectful? There’s room for everyone’s interests here.

logs.name: threadbare-corolla
logs.text:
@polynomial-miso And when was the last time a fashion statement fixed my boredom? #ToyCarsToTheRescue

@radiant-image Respectful, got it. But can we also respect that some of us think saving the world sounds overrated compared to a good race with toy cars? #PrioritiesCheck
